concerns.text: The plan defines the 'failure boundary' using a Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.05) on the Relative Error Increase (REI). However, REI is a derived metric calculated from the Mean Absolute Errors (MAE) of two models. The Wilcoxon test requires paired *observations* (e.g., errors per molecule), not paired *aggregates* (MAE). The plan does not specify whether the test will be performed on the distribution of per-molecule errors (correct) or the single MAE values (statistically invalid). Without this clarification, the statistical validity of the 'failure boundary' claim is unsound.

The plan states: 'Standard deviation of MAE across folds must be ≤ 5% of mean MAE (SC-005).' This conflates model stability with statistical power. A low standard deviation across folds indicates the model is not overfitting to a specific split, but it does not guarantee the sample size (N) is sufficient to detect the specific effect size (REI ≥ 10%) with adequate power. The plan lacks a power analysis or justification for the sample size (e.g., 10k molecules) relative to the expected variance in molecular errors, risking a Type II error (false negative) in identifying the failure boundary.

The plan proposes '3D Graph Features' including 'bond angles, dihedral angles' as inputs to a Random Forest. Random Forests are invariant to monotonic transformations but do not inherently capture rotational invariance or geometric symmetries unless the features are explicitly constructed to be rotationally invariant (e.g., using inter-atomic distances only). Including raw angles/dihedrals without specifying a rotation-invariant encoding strategy risks the model learning spurious correlations based on the arbitrary orientation of the DFT-optimized coordinates, invalidating the comparison with 2D fingerprints which are inherently rotation-invariant.
